name: Corn and Bean Pudding
recipeIngredient: 4 drizzle Hot pepper sauce (or to taste)
4 cups Cooked kidney beans or brown beans
recipeInstructions:
This recipe is suitable for a gluten-free diet.
1. Heat oil over medium heat in a small nonstick skillet. Cook onion, stirring occasionally about 4 minutes or until translucent.
2. In a casserole dish sprayed with nonstick coating, beat eggs, milk, salt, and hot pepper sauce (or to taste) until smooth. Stir in beans, corn, and cooked onion. Mix well.
3. Place the casserole in a larger pan. Pour enough hot water into the larger pan to come 2 inches up the sides.
4. Bake at 350°F for 20 minutes. Stir the mixture and bake an additional 20 minutes or until a tester inserted near the center comes out clean.
1/6 recipe - 181 calories, 1 1/2 starches, 1 protein choice, 3 grams total fat, 1 gram saturated fat, 74 mg cholesterol, 9 grams protein, 29 grams carbohydrate, 376 mg sodium, 389 mg potassium. Very high in fiber.
Tex-Mex variation: Add 1/3 cup chopped peppers or 2 teaspoons chopped pickled jalapeno pepper.
Adapted from Full of Beans by V. Currie & Kay Spicer (1993) ISBN 0-9695688-1-9 Mighton House, Box 399, Campbellville, Ont. L0P 1B0 Shared but not tested by Elizabeth Rodier Feb 94.
